module.title: SAP TM Freight Settlement & Charge Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand end-to-end settlement lifecycle
learningObjectives.description: Explain complete flow from POD through charge, verification, posting, and payment.

learningObjectives.title: Understand system integration
learningObjectives.description: Describe SAP TM and SAP S/4HANA finance integration across settlement stages.

learningObjectives.title: Understand settlement success factors
learningObjectives.description: Clarify master data, pricing setup, automation, and monitoring requirements.

sections.content: Freight Settlement is the final phase of the SAP Transportation Management lifecycle. It begins after transportation execution is completed and Proof of Delivery is confirmed. SAP TM calculates transportation charges, validates carrier invoices, creates Freight Settlement Documents, integrates with SAP S/4HANA Finance and completes carrier payment. This process ensures accurate financial reconciliation between logistics operations and accounting.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: The SAP TM lifecycle ends only after Freight Settlement is completed and the carrier has been financially settled.

A retail company delivers products to 500 stores daily. After delivery confirmation, SAP TM automatically calculates transportation charges using Freight Agreements, generates Freight Settlement Documents, verifies carrier invoices, posts accounting entries to SAP S/4HANA Finance and schedules electronic payments through Accounts Payable.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Automate the entire Freight Settlement lifecycle by integrating SAP TM with SAP S/4HANA Finance, electronic invoicing and carrier portals. This minimizes manual intervention and improves settlement accuracy.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A multinational FMCG company automated settlement for over 50,000 Freight Orders each month. Automatic charge calculation, invoice verification and financial integration reduced settlement time from ten days to less than two days while significantly improving carrier satisfaction.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Be able to explain the complete Freight Settlement flow: Freight Order → Proof of Delivery → Charge Calculation → Freight Agreement → Freight Settlement Document → Invoice Verification → Financial Posting → Accounts Payable → Carrier Payment.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Many beginners think Freight Settlement ends with FSD creation. In reality, the business process concludes only after invoice verification, financial posting and successful carrier payment.

items: Freight Settlement is the final SAP TM business process.
Freight Settlement begins after Proof of Delivery.
Charge Calculation uses Freight Agreements and Rate Tables.
Freight Settlement Documents integrate SAP TM with SAP S/4HANA Finance.
Invoice Verification validates carrier invoices before payment.
Accounts Payable processes the final carrier payment.
The end-to-end process ensures complete logistics and finance integration.
